info.name: easyjob Reference - Simple
info.description: With the **easyjob WebApi Service**, data is retrieved and updated in easyjob in real-time. The data is transferred in JSON format. In most cases with **GET** and **POST** requests. Access rights are assigned in the user manager in easyjob. All assigned rights can be requested with `{webapiurl}/api.json/Common/GetWebSettings` Always use the Header `ej-webapi-client: ThirdParty` to get access to all possible functions like refresh token. ### Use Postman to execute request on your own easyjob WebApi - Download and install the Postman App. [Download here](https://www.postman.com/downloads/) - Click on "Run in Postman" - Click on "Postman for Windows" - Select a workspace in which the easyjob WebApi Collection should be imported and click "import" - In the top right corner select in the drop down "Local Environment". Click on the eye icon and edit the 'webapiurl', 'username' and 'password' variable. - The first step is always to get an access token. Click on the left side on "Get Token" and click on "Send"

request.description:
Authorization takes place via the OAuth 2.0 Bearer Token. It must first be requested at the end point "/token" in the WebApi.

The request to this endpoint must be performed as an **HTTP-POST request.**  
The “Content-Type” header must be set as follows: "**Content-Type: application/x-www-form-urlencoded; charset=UTF-8**".  
The user name and password are transferred in the Request-Body with the following structure: grant_type=password&username=**Administrator**&password=**123**”

Authorization takes place both via the user's easyjob login or via the login data in the address/contact (WebApi license required).

The response contains a validity date next to the Access Token that indicates until when the token is valid ("expires_in" is indicated in seconds)

The received token must be set in all further requests in the Authorization Header: “**Authorization: Bearer ACCESS_TOKEN**”.

request.description:
With this POST Request it is possible to open different objects in an easyjob instance. **\-IMPORTANT-** This request is executed on behalf of the logged in webapi easyjob user (access token). This means that the easyjob WebApi Service will try to find an active easyjob instance where exactly this user is logged in. **Possible commands** | **Command** | **Description** | | --- | --- | | easyjob:OpenAddress?id=123 | Open address with ID 123 | | easyjob:OpenContact?id=123 | Open contact with ID 123 | | easyjob:OpenLead?id=123 | Open lead with ID 123 | | easyjob:OpenItem?id=123
easyjob:OpenAvailCheck?id=123 | Open item or availability of an item with ID 123 | | easyjob:OpenProject?id=123 | Open project with ID 123 | | easyjob:OpenJob?id=123
easyjob:OpenBillOfItems?id=123 | Open job or bill of items of an job with ID 123 | | easyjob:OpenInvoice?id=123 | Open invoice with ID 123 | | easyjob:OpenService?id=123 | Open service with ID 123 | | easyjob:OpenPurchaseOrder?id=123 | Open purchase order with ID 123 | | easyjob:OpenPurchaseInvoice?id=123 | Open invoice with ID 123 | | easyjob:OpenActivity?id=123 | Open activity with ID 123 | For most commands you can also use "Number" instead of "ID" to identify an easyjob object. For example: easyjob:OpenJob?number=22-0123
[More information about eTable here](#c30d6d34-3d88-4137-8b06-d3c7458b44c4)

_(available in easyjob version >= 6.20.2.56)_

_Purchase Order permissions needed: Edit permission and view price permission._

_The granted permissions be requested with /PurchaseOrders/Details/?id=idpurchaseorder with the fields "AllowHeadEdit" and "AllowPriceView"_

##### Field 'Fixed'

If set to 'True': the provided DiscountFactor will be fixed and it is not possible anymore to change the total price!

If set to 'False': the provided DiscountFactor will be applied but the total price can still be changed.

##### Field 'DiscountFactor'

Valid values are in the range between 0 and 1.

Example: DiscountFactor = 0.2 equals 20% discount of the total price.

item.description:
#### Authorization

The Authorization Header must be added to all subsequent requests.

[See 2. Request User Settings for more information](#800eafcc-e5df-4e40-bb98-289bc3170b52)

#### Date, time and timezones

Date and time are formatted in ISO 8601 (JSON Format) but don't have the UTC designator.

Example:

`2021-12-15T17:33`

#### What is IdTable (eTable)?

Some objects like CRM activities can refer to other easyjob objects like project, jobs, items etc.

IdTable (sometimes eTable) identify the type of the easyjob object.

| **IdTable** | **Description** |
| --- | --- |
| 1 | Address |
| 2 | Item / Artikel |
| 3 | Project |
| 4 | Job |
| 6 | Invoice |
| 9 | Resource |
| 10 | Device / Gerät |
| 12 | Contact |
| 17 | Purchase Order |
| 22 | Attachment / Documents |
| 23 | Purchase Invoice |
| 33 | GenericResource / Arbeitsmittel |
| 34 | Activity |
| 73 | Room |
| 74 | Room Configuration |
| 75 | Room Use Type |
| 76 | Room Occupancy |
| 77 | Room Use |
| 102 | Workflow Message |

item.description:
## General

The following provides a general explanation of how the endpoints can be used, the endpoints are very similar for each object.

#### /Grid - Table / List of Objects

**Columns -** _Array of Objects_

Defines the metadata for each column in the table.

Each object includes information about the column such as:  
**Name**: Internal name of the column (used for mapping data).  
**Caption**: Display name of the column header (can be null if not set).  
**Format**: Expected data type or format

**Data -** _Array of Arrays_

Represents the actual data content in tabular format.

Each inner array corresponds to one row in the table.

The order and number of values in each row correspond to the column definitions in the Columns array.

#### /FilterData

Return additional information for Filters that can be passed to /Grid.

#### /FormData

Return additional information for Form Elements in the GUI for one specific object.

#### /Details

Return information/fields of one specific object.

#### /Save

Save field of one specific object.

#### /SaveGridRow

Save changes of one table row
